questions.prompt: Which is correct for 'It's one o'clock'?
questions.explanation: Only 1:00 uses Es la una — all other hours use Son las.

questions.prompt: What does 'el fin de semana' mean?
questions.explanation: El fin de semana = the weekend (literally 'the end of the week').
Y media means half past. Son las ocho y media = 8:30.

questions.prompt: Days of the week in Spanish are...
questions.explanation: Unlike English, days of the week and months are not capitalized in Spanish unless they start a sentence.

questions.prompt: What is the Mexican word that can mean 'right now' OR 'in a while'?
questions.explanation: Ahorita is uniquely Mexican — its meaning (now, soon, or eventually) depends entirely on context and tone.
Agosto = August. Remember: months are not capitalized in Spanish.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'at three in the afternoon'?
options: a las tres de la tarde
es la tres de la tarde
a la tres por la tarde
questions.explanation: A las tres de la tarde = at three in the afternoon. Use a las for 'at [time]'.
The week ends: ...viernes, sábado, domingo. Sábado (Saturday) comes before domingo (Sunday).
